payload.input: She smuggles it inside wrapped in her Bible cover, charging it beneath her pillow while she feigns sleep, the heat of the charging port burning against her cheek like a coal. When the screen blinks to life at 2:00 AM, the notification icon explodes—247 unread messages, 18,000 new followers, a banner announcing "TRENDING: 3 ON CARNALCRYPT" in pulsing red letters. The site’s name glows against the black background, the word "Carnal" in serif font like a university press, "Crypt" in something jagged and Gothic beneath it, a digital mausoleum where the living post their deaths. Maya scrolls with her thumb, the light painting her face in shifting blues. Their video—the kitchen counter scene, the wedding photo visible in the background—has 340,000 views. The comments scroll in a manic river:  "Real siblings confirmed, look at the nose bridge genetics" "she’s so tight, can’t be older than 16" "post the anal training already" "ScholarlyPursuits is right, this is art"
